Relative to the wells, Mr Eyre (vol. i. p. 209) mentions he saw one called "Beel-im Gaippe." "Gaippe" means water, (&H op, "water,") and the waters of this well may, in reference to the Baal superstitions, be the "waters of the Baal gods." The Baal is a demon known under the name of " Boyl-ya" in South Australia, and much dreaded by the natives. He is believed to be deformed at the extremities, and is always sitting cross-legged on the ground, or ferrying about in his canoe. He is a spirit of such dreaded and malignant power that there are certain sorcerers particularly.
